reasoning: FRED statistical analysis estimates a 30% chance of an increase in the short term (March 23), heavily weighted by the general downward linear trend over the last month. Furthermore, macroeconomic forecasts anticipate 10-year Treasury yields to decrease modestly over 2025 to around 4.14%, driven by expected Fed rate cuts later in the year. Because AAA corporate bond yields are highly correlated with Treasuries, they are also likely to drop. Thus, the probability of an increase from the current rather elevated baseline (~4.79%) is below 0.50 across all horizons. The estimates gradually rise toward 0.50 at longer horizons to account for increasing uncertainty around long-term inflation and monetary policy.
